rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN PANARELLI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court is to determine whether to take this petition under submission at this time despite some unresolved issues, given that the petition has been pending for more than a year. Specifically, the Court is to determine whether: 1) Charles Madrid is an heir entitled to distribution of 1/240, despite the administrator's allegation that Charles Madrid has been missing since 2000 and should be presumed to be dead in Colorado; 2) Michael Madrid Sr. is an heir entitled to distribution of 1/240, absent proof that Charles Madrid, Michael Madrid Sr's sibling and missing heir, predeceased the decedent; 3) Distribution of Charles Madrid's 1/240 share should be ordered to be made to Michael Madrid Sr. as the sole alternate distributee under Probate Code section 11603(c) only if Charles Madrid continues to be missing for 5 years after the Court orders final distribution; 4) David Lopez is a postdeceased heir whose vested share of distribution is 1/24; 5) Distribution of David Lopez's 1/24 share should be ordered to be made to his surviving spouse, Mary Cooley, as the sole successor in interest, only if a spousal property order or small estate affidavit is on file at the time of final distribution; 6) Frank Lopez Jr. is a postdeceased heir whose vested share of distribution is 1/24; 7) Distribution of Frank Lopez Jr.'s 1/24 share should be ordered to be made to Kevin Lopez and Terrance Lopez, as the sole successors in interest, only if a small estate affidavit is on file at the time of final distribution; and 8) Waive supporting documentation corroborating Denise Lopez's child-parent relationship with Frank Lopez, accept the verified family tree listing Denise as one of Frank's children, and determine that Denis Lopez is an heir entitled to distribution of 1/24. Note: The amended petition incorrectly stated the proposed shares of Michael Sandoval, Michelle Marquez, Maria Rojas, Jesus Barrera, and Marisela Escalante. Their correct shares should be 1/480 each (instead of 1/48 as stated). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIGUEL Q URIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/22/24. (No earlier availability.) 1. The "supplement" filed on 4/2/24 (but not e-filed until 4/4/24) AMENDED the share of some of the assets on hand. 2. An amending pleading requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] 3. The only proper form of notice for Probate accountings is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing (DE-120), on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. 4. In any event, since the "supplement" that amended the ending balance was not e-filed until 4/4/24, notice would have been insufficient for the hearing on 4/15/24. 5. Notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is e-filed. [LRSF 14.14 A.] 6. Notice must be sent directly to an interested party. If an interested party is represented by an attorney, notice must ALSO be given to the attorney but not only to the attorney, unless the interested party has directed that notice be given only to the attorney. [CRC 7.51(a) and (b) and Probate Code Section 1214.] 7. An interested party must have served notice that the party accepts electronic service. [CRC 2.251(b).] No such consents appear to have been filed. 8. A Substitution of Attorney was filed on 2/15/24, indicating that Brenda Schenthal is now self-represented. 9. The total market value of the non-cash assets as of 3/22/23 is still alleged to be the same as the carry value (and market value) as of 4/11/22, which seems highly unlikely and is not supported by the Schwab statement. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/13/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (NB: Courtesy copies are file-stamped single-sided hard copies.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHERINE CAVNESS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/22/24. (1) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) The proof of service filed on 3/27/24 shows the incorrect street address for Maurice Cavness, the incorrect zip code for David A. Wallace, and an address for Dina Wallace Stewart that differs from the one alleged in the amended petition to be hers. (3) The verification of the amended petition did not verify the AMENDED petition. Deadline is 5/13/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PERRY L. AND GLORIA J. WASH FAMILY TRUST DATED FEB TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Judicial Determination Of Subtrust Allocation, For Order Confirming Effect Of Exercise Of Power Of Appointment, For Order Confirming Distributive Shares To Beneficiaries, And For Order Determining Vadility Of Trust Amendement
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: Petitioner makes requests pertaining to not only the family trust, but also the survivor's trust. A stipulation has been filed agreeing to adjudicate all issues in the separates trusts in this matter. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PIK KAU LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/22/24. (No earlier availability.) The Notice of Petition to Administer Estate must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall. Proof of service must be filed, which shows that notice with those instructions attached to the notice was given to the other interested parties. Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any other filed papers to the assigned examiner by 5/13/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (You are strongly encouraged to hire an attorney.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JEROLD AND BARBARA W. BABIN TRUST U/D/T DATED DECEMBER 11, 1998 AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Modify Irrevocable Trust To Remove Mandatory Allocation Of Assets On Death Of First Spouse And Prohibition On Amendment
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/22/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) The supplement to complete the petition was not filed until 4/8/24, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. Either notice must be given with a copy of the supplement or waivers of notice of the petition, as supplemented, must be filed. (3) Some pages (22-23) of the trust are missing from the copy attached to the supplement. (4) Does the proposed modification have any effect on the amendment to the trust? (5) Were the remainder beneficiaries advised by separate counsel? Are they aware that revoking the limitations on amending or revoking all or any portion of the trust could mean that their mother could change the remainder beneficiaries? (6) It appears that a proposed order was just lodged on 4/9/24. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/13/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NEHA MALLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate will be set for 4/28/2025. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 4/28/2025 if no petition has been filed.
